title: Dog Licked Topical Pain Relief Cream: Emergency Guide
meta_desc: If your dog licked pain relief cream, it is a high-urgency emergency. Learn why NSAIDs like Diclofenac are toxic and how to help your dog now.
content_html:
What happened?

Topical pain relief creams, such as those used for arthritis or muscle soreness, often contain active ingredients like Flurbiprofen, Diclofenac, Salicylates (found in products like Bengay or Icy Hot), or Lidocaine. While these are common in human medicine, they are processed differently by dogs. Even a small amount licked off a person's skin or directly from a tube can lead to severe poisoning.

Is it an emergency?

Urgency Level: High. Yes, this should be treated as a veterinary emergency. Ingredients like Flurbiprofen are extremely potent in canines; ingestion of even trace amounts can lead to rapid-onset kidney failure or life-threatening gastrointestinal ulceration. Because damage can occur internally before you see physical symptoms, immediate professional intervention is required to prevent permanent organ damage.

Why a photo helps triage

When you call your veterinarian or an animal poison control hotline, they will need to know the exact concentration of the active ingredients. Taking a clear photo of the front and back (the drug facts label) of the tube or packaging allows the triage team to calculate the potential toxic dose based on your dog's weight. This information is vital for determining whether your dog needs aggressive hospitalization or outpatient decontamination.

faqs.question: Can I wait to see if my dog acts sick?
faqs.answer: No. Many toxins in pain creams cause silent internal damage to the kidneys and stomach lining. By the time a dog shows symptoms like vomiting or lethargy, significant damage may have already occurred.

faqs.question: Should I induce vomiting at home?
faqs.answer: Do not induce vomiting unless specifically directed by a veterinarian. Depending on the ingredients and how long ago the ingestion occurred, inducing vomiting could cause further complications.

faqs.question: What are the common signs of pain cream toxicity?
faqs.answer: Common signs include vomiting (sometimes with blood), loss of appetite, abdominal pain, black or tarry stools, and increased thirst or urination.
